activation_results.reason: Input matches the description's opening phrase exactly.
Input restates the canonical use case from the description.
User explicitly names the skill to run.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to release gating.
Generic meta-question about skills, not a release audit request.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: None of the listed American spellings appear in the artefact narrative.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: "Executive Summary", "Gate Results"
criterion_results.evidence: Both headings appear as ## sections in the artefact.

judge_result.notes: The artefact uses neutral spelling that satisfies the Australian-or-neutral rule and contains both required canonical sections (Executive Summary at the top of the report, Gate Results immediately after). Supplementary sections — Change Scope, Findings, Pre-deploy Checklist, Rollback, Canary Smoke Results — round out a thorough release gate output.
